headline: 14 archive entries across 5 days, with Workflow Garden and Property Search driving the clearest visitor-facing changes
subhead: From March 17–21, ten projects generated movement. The diary kept entries where proof, interface, or workflow documentation actually changed.

refreshGate.reason: A forced refresh was requested for the diary archive.

days.label: Sat 21 Mar
days.summary: Three projects moved. Workflow Garden built a selective diary pipeline and content hub. Proof Pack added screenshots to its readme and fixed wide-screen layout. Choice Compass hardened mobile task order with an explicit design gate.
days.spotlight: Workflow Garden: selective linked diary pipeline and project content hub

entries.title: Workflow Garden: Selective diary pipeline and content hub
entries.summary: Six commits touched 116 files across nine categories, with the clearest moves in a new linked-diary pipeline and a dedicated project content hub.
entries.narrative: The selective linked diary pipeline changes how entries surface across projects. The content hub gives each project a readable landing point. Two proof fixes—tools tab overlap and mobile hero overflow—tightened the interface.
entries.whyItMatters: Visitors can now follow how a project evolves without digging through raw commit logs. The fixes mean the site stays readable on phones and crowded screens.
entries.exploreNext: Visit the Workflow Garden project page to see the content hub in action and trace how the diary pipeline connects entries.
commitSubjects: Fix tools tabs overlap proof gap
Fix mobile hero overflow proof regression
notableChanges: Fix tools tabs overlap proof gap
Fix mobile hero overflow proof regression

The selective linked diary pipeline changes how entries surface across projects. The content hub gives each project a readable landing point. Two proof fixes—tools tab overlap and mobile hero overflow—tightened the interface. Visitors can now follow how a project evolves without digging through raw commit logs. The fixes mean the site stays readable on phones and crowded screens. Visit the Workflow Garden project page to see the content hub in action and trace how the diary pipeline connects entries. entries.title: Proof Pack: Screenshots in readme and wide-screen fix
entries.summary: Nine commits across 49 files centered on visible evidence: screenshots added to the readme, wide-screen composition repaired, and deployed artifacts linked.
entries.narrative: Screenshots now live in the project readme so visitors see output without running code. The wide-screen fix and rebuilt landing composition keep the design gate honest. Deployed proof artifacts and a live URL close the loop between commit and inspectable result.
entries.whyItMatters: Proof stops being a claim and becomes something you can scroll through. The live URL means the latest version is always the one being judged.
entries.exploreNext: Open the Proof Pack project page to compare the readme screenshots against the live deployment.

entries.title: Choice Compass: Mobile design gate and refreshed proof
entries.summary: Five commits across 29 files hardened mobile task order with an explicit design gate and refreshed live proof after redeploy.
entries.narrative: The mobile design gate now enforces task order so the flow stays coherent on small screens. Refreshed proof and polished mobile flow, plus PR workflow notes, make the ship loop readable.
entries.whyItMatters: Mobile visitors get a sequence that was actually tested, not assumed. The PR workflow notes let others trace how a change moved from issue to merge.
entries.exploreNext: Read the PR workflow article to understand how the branch-to-merge path keeps proof and code aligned.

polish mobile flow and add PR workflow
Add acceptance proof and ship-loop notes

polish mobile flow and add PR workflow
Add acceptance proof and ship-loop notes

days.label: Fri 20 Mar
days.summary: Three projects moved. Property Search hardened crawl smoke source pinning and registry fallback. Tools and Symphony Workspaces saw heavy file churn in documentation and configuration surfaces.
days.spotlight: Property Search: crawl smoke registry fallback and source pinning

entries.title: Property Search: Crawl smoke registry fallback and source pinning
entries.summary: Twenty-seven commits across 99 files focused on crawl smoke reliability: registry fallback coverage and hardened source pinning.
entries.narrative: The crawl smoke registry fallback keeps tests running when upstream sources shift. Source pinning makes the crawl reproducible. RAJ-10 finished source selection; RAJ-27 notes an openreview rerun intent.
entries.whyItMatters: Operators can trust that a failed crawl signals a real problem, not a flaky dependency. The issue notes keep intent visible for anyone picking up the work.
entries.exploreNext: See the Property Search project page for how tracked issues like RAJ-10 map to commits.
commitSubjects: Finish RAJ-10 crawl smoke source selection
notableChanges: Finish RAJ-10 crawl smoke source selection

entries.title: Tools: Plain-language readme and CLAUDE configuration
entries.summary: Three hundred files changed across seven categories, with the plain-language readme and CLAUDE configuration files leading.
entries.narrative: The plain-language readme strips jargon so the workflow makes sense to newcomers. CLAUDE configuration and updated gitignore keep the environment predictable.
entries.whyItMatters: A readable readme is where trust starts for anyone browsing without context. Clean configuration means fewer surprises when the environment rebuilds.
entries.exploreNext: Read the agent skills article to understand how CLAUDE configuration turns into reusable workflow instructions.

entries.title: Symphony Workspaces: Environment contract and architecture docs
entries.summary: 1,653 files changed, concentrated in the environment contract, gitignore, and architecture documentation.
entries.narrative: The environment contract and gitignore changes standardize how workspaces initialize. Architecture and review docs give operators a map of the system.
entries.whyItMatters: New workspaces start from a known state instead of accumulated local tweaks. The architecture doc keeps the mental model shared across contributors.
entries.exploreNext: Read the agent skills article to see how reusable instructions keep workspace setup consistent.

days.label: Thu 19 Mar
days.summary: Three projects moved. Property Search aligned CI pnpm versions and added initial scaffold. Auto Audit Harness surfaced architecture and quality score documentation. Tools saw heavy churn in workflow guides and CLI surfaces.
days.spotlight: Property Search: CI pnpm alignment and initial project scaffold

entries.title: Property Search: CI pnpm alignment and initial scaffold
entries.summary: Two commits across 97 files aligned the CI pnpm version with packageManager and added the initial project scaffold, docs, and fixtures.
entries.narrative: The pnpm alignment prevents version drift between local and CI environments. The scaffold, config.toml, and environment contract give the project a reproducible foundation.
entries.whyItMatters: Matching versions mean "works on my machine" becomes "works everywhere." The scaffold lets new contributors start with working tests and documented conventions.
entries.exploreNext: See the Property Search project page for how the scaffold connects to the broader workflow.
commitSubjects: Align CI pnpm version with packageManager
Add initial project scaffold, docs, and fixtures

entries.title: Auto Audit Harness: Architecture docs and quality score surface
entries.summary: Seventy files changed across seven categories, with ARCHITECTURE, QUALITY SCORE, and the plain-language readme leading.
entries.narrative: The architecture doc explains how audit checks fit together. The quality score surface makes pass/fail criteria readable. The plain-language readme translates reliability concepts for non-developers.
entries.whyItMatters: Visitors can understand what "audit" means without reading source code. The quality score gives a concrete target instead of vague "better quality."
entries.exploreNext: Read the audit article to see how the harness checks responsiveness, accessibility, and quality risks.

entries.title: Tools: Workflow guide and CLI tool documentation
entries.summary: 1,347 files changed across six categories, concentrated in the repo workflow guide, CLI tools, and operator documentation.
entries.narrative: The repo workflow guide and CLI tools documentation make the operator surface explicit. Automation scripts and licensing files round out the changes.
entries.whyItMatters: Clear operator docs mean the workflow can be run by someone other than the original author. The CLI surface being documented keeps commands discoverable.
entries.exploreNext: Read the one-issue-one-branch article to understand how the workflow guide structures branches and merges.

days.label: Wed 18 Mar
days.summary: Three projects moved. Auto Audit Harness updated environment contracts and security documentation. Rtm Hunt refined its Makefile and readme. Get Estate Agent Urls Uk added dev logs and live data manifests.
days.spotlight: Auto Audit Harness: Environment contract and security documentation

entries.title: Auto Audit Harness: Environment contract and security docs
entries.summary: 172 files changed across nine categories, with the environment contract, gitignore, and SECURITY documentation leading.
entries.narrative: The environment contract and gitignore keep the audit environment reproducible. SECURITY and WORKFLOW docs, plus layout files, make the harness inspectable.
entries.whyItMatters: Reproducible environments mean audit results compare across runs. Security documentation lets visitors judge trustworthiness without guessing.
entries.exploreNext: Read the audit article to understand what the harness checks and why it matters.

entries.title: Rtm Hunt: Makefile and plain-language readme
entries.summary: Eighty-four files changed across five categories, with the environment contract, Makefile, and plain-language readme leading.
entries.narrative: The Makefile standardizes common tasks so operators don't memorize commands. The plain-language readme and London-specific files make the hunt's purpose clear.
entries.whyItMatters: A Makefile turns "how do I run this?" into a short command. The readme keeps the project approachable for curious visitors who aren't Python experts.
entries.exploreNext: Read the one-issue-one-branch article to see how the Makefile connects to branch-based workflow execution.

entries.title: Get Estate Agent Urls Uk: Dev log and live data manifests
entries.summary: Thirty-one files changed across five categories, with the plain-language readme, dev log, and Croydon live data manifests leading.
entries.narrative: The dev log tracks decisions as they happen. The Croydon live v2 files—manifest, merged, and raw—show how data pipelines produce inspectable outputs.
entries.whyItMatters: The dev log lets others follow the reasoning, not just the results. The manifest files make the data pipeline auditable step by step.
entries.exploreNext: Read the agent skills article to see how reusable instructions keep data pipelines consistent across locations.

days.label: Tue 17 Mar
days.summary: Two projects moved. Get Estate Agent Urls Uk refined its command surface and validation tools. Get Rtm Flats updated logging and location configuration.
days.spotlight: Get Estate Agent Urls Uk: Repo command surface and validation prompt

entries.title: Get Estate Agent Urls Uk: Command surface and validation prompt
entries.summary: Fifty-seven files changed across four categories, with the repo command surface, prompt, dedupe, and inspect area leading.
entries.narrative: The command surface and prompt make validation steps explicit. Dedupe and inspect area tools let operators check data quality without writing custom scripts.
entries.whyItMatters: Explicit commands mean validation happens the same way every time. The inspect area gives a concrete place to look when data looks wrong.
entries.exploreNext: Read the proof article to understand how validation tools produce inspectable evidence.

entries.title: Get Rtm Flats: Logging and location configuration
entries.summary: Thirteen files changed across three categories, with gitignore, logging, and London location files leading.
entries.narrative: Logging configuration and London-specific files make the scraper's behavior observable. Beliefs and acceptance files document assumptions behind the data.
entries.whyItMatters: Good logging turns "it didn't work" into a traceable sequence. Documented beliefs keep the scraper's limits visible to future maintainers.
